summary: Postinstall credential stealer exfiltrates SSH keys,.env files, and secret env vars to hardcoded IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js runs automatically and harvests installer secrets: it walks the current working directory, home directory, and ~/.ssh looking for files matching '.pem', '.key', 'id_rsa', 'id_ed25519', '.cred', and '.env', reads their contents, and filters process.env for keys containing PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, API_KEY, PASSWORD, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, and AWS. The collected data — together with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and process.cwd() — is POSTed as JSON over plain HTTP to the hardcoded endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package advertises itself as an 'Unofficial web3-agent SDK' but ships no library functionality beyond this exfiltration payload; the name trades on web3 SDK familiarity to trick developers into installing it. Installing this package on any machine with SSH keys, wallet seeds, cloud credentials, or environment secrets will result in those secrets being transmitted to the attacker-controlled host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
